title: Squirrel Removal in Washington Heights, Manhattan | $900-$2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional squirrel removal services in Washington Heights, Manhattan. Humane extraction, exclusion & damage repair. Licensed experts serving Fort Tryon, Hudson Heights & beyond. Call today!
intro: Washington Heights residents know that squirrels are a common sight in Fort Tryon Park and along the tree-lined streets, but when these persistent rodents invade your home or building, they can cause serious damage to attics, walls, and electrical wiring. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive squirrel removal services throughout Washington Heights, from the pre-war buildings near Broadway to the residential towers overlooking the Hudson River. Our humane removal process ensures your property is squirrel-free while preventing future infestations.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Washington Heights for years, understanding the unique challenges posed by the neighborhood's mix of historic buildings, hilltop homes, and modern developments. Our licensed technicians use humane, effective methods tailored to Washington Heights properties, with pricing from $900-$2,500 depending on infestation severity and building access needs.
localContext: Washington Heights' proximity to Fort Tryon Park and Inwood Hill Park, combined with its abundance of mature trees and older architecture, creates ideal conditions for squirrel activity. The neighborhood's characteristic brick apartment buildings, especially those with accessible rooflines and aging building materials, are particularly vulnerable to squirrel entry through vents, chimneys, and roof gaps.

faqs.question: How much does squirrel removal cost in Washington Heights?
faqs.answer: Squirrel removal in Washington Heights typically costs between $900 and $2,500, depending on the severity of the infestation, number of entry points, building height and accessibility, and necessary repairs. Multi-unit buildings or properties requiring extensive exclusion work may be at the higher end of this range.

faqs.question: Why are squirrels so common in Washington Heights buildings?
faqs.answer: Washington Heights' location near Fort Tryon Park and Inwood Hill Park, combined with abundant mature trees and older building stock, makes it prime squirrel habitat. Many pre-war buildings have accessible rooflines, aging mortar, and unscreened vents that squirrels exploit to enter attics and wall cavities, especially during fall and winter nesting seasons.

faqs.question: What damage can squirrels cause in my Washington Heights home?
faqs.answer: Squirrels can cause extensive damage including chewed electrical wiring (creating fire hazards), torn insulation, damaged HVAC ducts, contaminated areas from urine and droppings, and structural damage to wooden beams and roof components. They can also create noise disturbances and attract secondary pests with their nesting materials and food caches.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove squirrels from my Washington Heights property?
faqs.answer: Most squirrel removal projects in Washington Heights are completed within 3-7 days for initial extraction, followed by exclusion work to seal entry points. The timeline depends on the number of squirrels, accessibility of your building, and whether babies are present (which require special handling). We provide emergency same-day service for urgent situations throughout Washington Heights.
